data.title: Benchmark: Delay in the crypto market structure bill could lead to structural limitations on the US crypto market.
data.description: Wall Street brokerage Benchmark stated that the US cryptocurrency market will face structural constraints if Congress fails to pass a market structure bill this year. Analyst Mark Palmer noted in a report that the legislative vacuum will lead to a persistent structural risk premium, limiting the valuation expansion of platforms influenced by the US. This situation will delay the maturation of cryptocurrencies, making investors more inclined towards Bitcoin-centric investments, strong balance sheets, and cash flow-generating infrastructure, rather than regulatory-sensitive areas such as exchanges, decentralized finance (DeFi), and altcoins. DeFi and smart contract platforms are most vulnerable, while Bitcoin, miners, and energy-backed infrastructure are less risky. The bill aims to define market structure by specifying how digital assets are classified as commodities or securities and clarifying the regulatory responsibilities of the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). Despite the delays, Palmer believes the bill is more likely to pass than fail, and any version of the legislation will reduce regulatory risks and unlock broader institutional participation. (CoinDesk)

data.title: U.S. Crypto Market Faces Structural Constraints Without Congressional Action
data.description: Benchmark, a Wall Street brokerage firm, has indicated that the U.S. cryptocurrency market will face structural limitations if Congress fails to pass a market structure bill this year. According to Odaily, analyst Mark Palmer highlighted in a report that the absence of legislation will lead to a persistent structural risk premium, thereby restricting valuation expansion for platforms influenced by the U.S. This situation is expected to delay the maturation of cryptocurrencies, prompting investors to favor Bitcoin-centric investments, robust balance sheets, and cash flow-generating infrastructure over exchanges, decentralized finance (DeFi), and altcoins, which are sensitive to regulatory changes. DeFi and smart contract platforms are particularly vulnerable, whereas Bitcoin, miners, and energy-supported infrastructure face less risk. The proposed bill aims to define how digital assets are classified as commodities or securities and clarify the regulatory roles of the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). Despite potential delays, Palmer believes the likelihood of the bill passing is greater than not, and any version of the legislation would reduce regulatory risks and encourage broader institutional participation.

data.title: First Avalanche ETF hits US markets as VanEck launches AVAX fund
data.description:
The first fund to track the Avalanche's AVAX token and include staking rewards debuted Monday with the launch of the VanEck Avalanche ETF.
source: https://www.theblock.co/post/387048/first-avalanche-etf-hits-us-markets-as-vaneck-launches-avax-fund?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: US Senate Banking Committee Delays CLARITY Act Markup Session
data.description: The U.S. Senate Banking Committee has rescheduled the markup session for the CLARITY Act, a legislative proposal concerning the structure of the cryptocurrency market. According to NS3.AI, the session is now set for January 29 at 3:30 p.m. UTC. Earlier reports suggested that the markup might be postponed until late February or March.

data.title: UK Banks Impose Restrictions on Crypto Transfers, Report Finds
data.description: A recent report highlights that approximately 40% of transfers from major UK banks to cryptocurrency exchanges are either blocked or delayed. According to NS3.AI, eight out of ten leading UK centralized exchanges have faced heightened restrictions over the past year. The UK Crypto-Business Council has criticized banks for leveraging regulatory compliance as a means to curb crypto financial activities, viewing these actions as obstacles to the growth of the industry.

data.title: Hong Kong Securities Regulator Flags Ju.com as Suspicious Platform
data.description: Hong Kong's Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) has officially added "Ju.com" to its list of suspicious virtual asset trading platforms. According to PANews, the SFC stated that the entity claims to operate a virtual asset trading platform and is suspected of conducting unlicensed activities.

data.title: BNB Chain's Prediction Markets Surpass $20 Billion in Trading Volume
data.description: BNB Chain has reported that its prediction markets have reached a cumulative trading volume exceeding $20 billion. According to NS3.AI, this milestone was shared in a post on the X platform, citing data from Dune Analytics. The significant trading volume highlights increased user participation and liquidity within BNB Chain's decentralized prediction market ecosystem.

data.title: Kraken launches "DeFi Earn" service in the US, EU, and Canada.
data.description: Kraken has launched its "DeFi Earn" service in the US, EU, and Canada, aiming to make DeFi earning opportunities more accessible to centralized exchange users. The product will offer on-chain earning opportunities while maintaining the "simplicity and security" expected of centralized exchanges. Kraken's DeFi Earn will be powered by vault infrastructure provider Veda, with risk management firms Chaos Labs and Sentora operating the first three USDC vaults. Funds will be allocated to on-chain protocols such as Aave, Morpho, Sky, and Tydro, which will provide floating yields "derived from genuine market demand for borrower payments." (The Block)

data.title: Crypto crash wipes $150 billion, pushes players into sports and politics bets
data.description:
A brutal crypto crash has erased $150 billion in value and driven traders into a different kind of gamble; sports, politics, and real-world prediction markets. People who used to trade memecoins now track election odds, weather forecasts, and even central bank decisions. Analyst Nikshep Saravanan reportedly used to spend his days building a digital creator […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/crypto-crash-pushes-sports-politics-bets/

data.title: Spot silver and New York silver futures both broke through the $111 mark.
data.description: Spot silver broke through $111/oz, rising 7.54% on the day and setting a new all-time high. New York silver futures also broke through $111/oz, rising 9.54% on the day. (Jinshi)

data.title: The U.S. Senate Agriculture Committee is scheduled to reconsider the cryptocurrency market structure bill this Thursday.
data.description: According to a post on the X platform by crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett, the U.S. Senate Agriculture Committee plans to reconsider the Crypto Markets Structure Act at 10:30 a.m. local time this Thursday.

data.description: On Jan 26, 2026, 14:50 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 88,000 USDT and is now trading at 87,976.773438 USDT, with a narrowed 0.77% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Morgan Stanley: Gold prices could rise to $5,700
data.description: Morgan Stanley predicts that gold prices are poised to rise to $5,700 per ounce in the second half of the year, driven by geopolitical uncertainty, continued central bank gold purchases, and strong ETF demand. The report notes that central banks worldwide (such as Poland) are increasing their gold reserves despite high prices. If the Federal Reserve initiates interest rate cuts in 2026, it could further support strong physical gold demand. (Gold10)

data.title: S&P 500 Index Reaches Weekly High with Continued Gains
data.description: The S&P 500 index has continued its upward trend, achieving a weekly high with a current increase of 0.4%. According to ChainCatcher, this marks a significant milestone for the index, reflecting ongoing positive momentum in the market.

data.title: Intel Shares Continue to Decline in Early U.S. Trading
data.description: Intel's stock experienced a further decline in early U.S. trading, dropping by 3.6%. According to ChainCatcher, this follows a downward trend that began last Friday.

data.title: Tezos Completes 20th Protocol Upgrade, Cuts Block Time to 6 Seconds
data.description:
Tezos activated its Tallinn protocol upgrade on January 24, 2026, reducing block time from 8 seconds to 6 seconds. The upgrade marks the blockchain's 20th forkless evolution through on-chain governance.
source: https://coinpaprika.com/news/tezos-tallinn-upgrade-slashes-block-time-6-seconds
